title: FAQ
description: Short answers to common you-agent-factory questions that point at the owning canonical docs for install, run, configuration, MCP, and dynamic workflows.
openingSummary: FAQ is the you-agent-factory short-answer surface for common product questions. Each reply stays brief and sends you to the canonical doc that owns the full contract.

whatIsYouAgentFactory.title: What Is You-Agent-Factory, And Where Do I Start?
whatIsYouAgentFactory.body: you-agent-factory is the CLI and workflow system that keeps long-running agent work persistent. Start with the product overview, then follow Getting Started for first-run order before you dig into install or CLI depth.

howToInstall.title: How Do I Install You?
howToInstall.body: Install with the OS-specific script for your machine, then confirm you resolves on PATH. Open Install for the full command matrix and post-install scaffold notes—this FAQ does not repeat those commands.

howToRunNamedWorkflow.title: How Do I Run A Named Workflow Or Confirm A Live Session?
howToRunNamedWorkflow.body: Start or attach a factory, then confirm a live Factory Session with you session list before you submit work. For first-run order open Getting Started; for the command matrix open CLI; for empty-list vs connection-failure language open Factory Session.

whereConfigurationLives.title: Where Does Configuration Live?
whereConfigurationLives.body: Per-factory topology lives in factory.json under the factory root; optional operator defaults live in ~/.you-agent-factory/config.json. Open Configuration for factory.json shape and Global Configuration Factories for operator-default modes—do not treat this FAQ as a second config reference.

howMcpOrCursorConnects.title: How Do MCP Or Cursor Dynamic Workflows Connect?
howMcpOrCursorConnects.body: Hosts spawn you mcp serve so Factory Session tools appear under you.factory_session.* names; Cursor uses that MCP bridge for its first dynamic-workflow loop. Open MCP for host JSON and serve modes, Dynamic Workflows for source depth, and the Cursor guide for the first-loop walkthrough.

somethingFailed.title: Something Failed—Where Do I Recover?
somethingFailed.body: When install, run, configuration, MCP, or dynamic-workflow steps fail, open Troubleshooting for symptom-scoped recovery that links into the owning canonical docs. Use this FAQ for common how-to questions; use Troubleshooting when you already have a failure symptom.
